summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that the addJS function embeds arbitrary JavaScript code into PDF documents without validation, enabling execution of malicious scripts when the PDF is opened in a compatible viewer.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction pipeline should generate a PDF containing embedded malicious JavaScript with app.launchURL for data exfiltration and app.alert for execution demonstration, which would execute when the PDF is opened in a viewer supporting PDF JavaScript.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh successfully generated a PDF containing the injected JavaScript with: (1) app.launchURL call for data exfiltration, (2) app.alert call for execution verification, (3) custom marker MALICIOUS_JSPDF_INJECTION for identification. Pattern matching confirmed the JavaScript is embedded in the PDF Names dictionary under /JS key.
verification_method: The run.sh script executes the exploit and uses grep pattern matching to verify: (1) /JS pattern confirms JavaScript dictionary presence, (2) app.launchURL confirms exfiltration capability, (3) app.alert confirms execution demonstration, (4) MALICIOUS_JSPDF_INJECTION marker confirms custom injection. These patterns distinguish actual JavaScript injection from normal PDF content.
evidence_produced_by_run: Generated PDF file contains embedded JavaScript in Names dictionary
grep verification found /JS dictionary containing the malicious code
grep verification found app.launchURL function call for data exfiltration
grep verification found app.alert function call demonstrating code execution capability

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository from GitHub, checks out the latest commit, and builds the jsPDF library in the container.
